children.label: A
children.text: the unreduced amount of the participant’s or retired participant’s annuity, as computed under ; orsection 2031(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the unreduced amount of what such annuity as so computed would be if the participant, former participant, or retired participant had not elected payment of the lump-sum credit under .section 2143 of this title

children.heading: Reduction in survivor annuity
children.label: 2 Reduction in survivor annuity
children.text: A survivor annuity payable under this section shall be reduced by an amount equal to any survivor annuity payments made to the former spouse under .section 2033 of this title

the former spouse remarries before age 55, except that the entitlement of the former spouse to such a survivor annuity shall be restored on the date such remarriage is dissolved by death, annulment, or divorce; or
the former spouse is less than 50 years of age.

children.heading: Commencement and termination of annuity
children.label: c Commencement and termination of annuity
i
children.text: the 60th day after such date; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date on which the former spouse reaches age 50; and

children.label: i
children.text: the date on which the participant or retired participant to whom the former spouse was married dies;

children.label: iii
children.text: the date on which the former spouse attains age 50.

children.heading: Termination of annuity
children.label: 2 Termination of annuity
children.text: The entitlement of a former spouse to a survivor annuity under this section terminates on the last day of the month before the former spouse’s death or remarriage before attaining age 55. The entitlement of a former spouse to such a survivor annuity shall be restored on the date such remarriage is dissolved by death, annulment, or divorce.

children.heading: Time limit; waiver
children.label: 1 Time limit; waiver
children.text: A survivor annuity under this section shall not be payable unless appropriate written application is provided to the Director, complete with any supporting documentation which the Director may by regulation require. Any such application shall be submitted not later than . The Director may waive the application deadline under the preceding sentence in any case in which the Director determines that the circumstances warrant such a waiver.April 1, 1989

children.heading: Retroactive benefits
children.label: 2 Retroactive benefits
children.text: Upon approval of an application provided under paragraph (1), the appropriate survivor annuity shall be payable to the former spouse with respect to all periods before such approval during which the former spouse was entitled to such annuity under this section, but in no event shall a survivor annuity be payable under this section with respect to any period before .October 1, 1986

children.heading: Restoration of annuity
children.label: e Restoration of annuity
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (d)(1), the deadline by which an application for a survivor annuity must be submitted shall not apply in cases in which a former spouse’s entitlement to such a survivor annuity is restored under subsection (b)(1) or (c)(2).
